name: web_search
description: Search the web via DuckDuckGo. Privacy-focused, no API key required.
documentation: Searches the web via DuckDuckGo and returns titles, snippets, URLs, and optional images. Use for general web queries when no domain-specific tool applies. For programming questions prefer stackexchange, for tech discussions prefer hackernews, for academic papers prefer arxiv, for news prefer news_tool, for encyclopedic knowledge prefer wikipedia. Supports time_range filtering (day/week/month/year). Pair with web_read to fetch full page content from promising results. DuckDuckGo may rate-limit on burst usage — space queries and prefer domain-specific tools to reduce pressure.

trigger.description: Initiated by Chalie when general web search is needed

query.description: Search query (e.g., 'best practices for Python async', 'latest news on AI regulation')

limit.description: Number of results to return (1-8). Capped at 8 to avoid cognitive overload.

time_range.description: Filter results by time: 'day', 'week', 'month', or 'year'. Omit for no filter.

_meta.description: Observability: fetch_latency_ms, source_count, unique_domains, has_images, retry_used

tips: Prefer stackexchange for programming/tech Q&A, hackernews for tech discussions, arxiv for papers, news_tool for news
Set time_range='day' or 'week' for recent results on fast-moving topics
Pair with web_read to fetch full content from the most relevant result
Use memorize to persist key findings as facts
